title: PA Compact
description: N/A - new project

title: First 5 Alameda County — Coordinated Child Care Eligibility & Enrollment System
description: N/A - new project

title: CAMP - Department of State
description: N/A - new project

title: ACF
description: Focus is leading this inititive to build an MVP version to modernize the grant outcome reporting system for ACF. We are initially targetung the tribal grant reporting forms for MVP. We will build on MVP learnings to build a generalized form builder and reporting platform across all ACF grant forms.

clientQuote.quote: It makes me so happy … in such a short time you’re able to gauge all this information and being able to support us. I think you’re not one step ahead, you guys are like 2-3 steps ahead and, I think some of the things you’re saying, I didn’t even know I needed, but now I’m convinced I need those.
clientQuote.name: Program Stakeholder, ACF Grants

title: Passport Adjudication Modernization
description: Our team is leading an initiative to modernize the Department of State's Consular Division passport adjudication process by building a new centralized, accessible, cloud-native adjudication platform MVP to eventually replace multiple on-premises legacy systems.

clientQuote.quote: Out of all the technologies I've seen in my time here, this system makes the most sense!
clientQuote.name: Regional Director, Passport Services, U.S. Departmnent of State

title: FAFSA Test Automation
description: Leading the initiative to build more resilience across mutli-vendor suite of backend and frontend systems at Federal Student Aid. Work is focused on standardizing automated test coverage and improving observability across user-facing and back-office systems.

title: Massachusetts Childcare Assistance
description: Our team is supporting the implementation of the Massachusetts childcare assistance program, which successfully launched Nov 2025.

title: Minnesota Paid Leave
description: Our team is supporting the full implementation of the Minnesota Paid Leave program, which successfully launched in January 2026. Ahead of the launch we made key contributions on the employer and claimaint portal, payments, adjudication, integration, digital certification, and testing teams. We continue to support this program post-launch through various enhahcements and multiple leave type expansions.

impact.headline: $180M in benefits approved
impact.description: Nearly 48,000 applications submitted and more than 20,000 approved within the first two months of launch.

impact.headline: Saved $70m
impact.description: The initial implementation was $70 million under budget and savings were redirected back into the claims pool.

title: DC Housing Insights
description: Focus is a maintainer of an open source tool providing housing advocates and government officials with insights on affordable housing in Washington, DC. Overlays multiple local and national data sources on an interactive map with filter toggles. Focus supports hosting, data updates, and feature development.

title: IRS CIO Strategic Initiatives
description: Supported the IRS CIO office in helping teams adopt a product-centric development approach, providing technical guidance and roadmaps. Embedded on the Intake team to modernize a legacy COBOL system and align multiple vendor teams around shared code quality standards.

title: Simpler Grants.gov
client: Dept of Health and Human Services
description: An open source modernization of the Grants.gov website and backend systems, aiming to make grant-seeking and applications seamless for eligible organizations while streamlining publishing and monitoring for grantor agencies. Focus supports the design and implementation effort across grant search and apply workstreams.

impact.headline: From 82 to 6 steps
impact.description: Significantly reduce the number of steps to create a grant seeker account.

title: IRS Digital Transformation
description: Led an agency-wide digital transformation at the IRS, conducting interviews across divisions to identify obstacles to digital delivery. Delivered a digital maturity assessment, a living roadmap, and two "beacon" pilot projects including digitizing the paper-based W7 form and improving compliance operations.

impact.headline: 4 months
impact.description: Our team digitized the W7 form in just four months to improve a paper form based process that typically takes weeks.

title: Healthcare.gov Marketplace
client: Center for Medicare and Medicaid Services
description: We are leading the product work for a suite of education tools that help consumers shop for healthcare plans and understand their coverage options. Our leaders bring extensive experience in product and engineering leadership on HealthCare.gov and MyMedicare.gov from prior engagements.

impact.headline: Fewer zero results searches
impact.description: Redesigned the Find Local Help search tool to more easily enable consumers to connect to helpers in their area and understand the key differences between types of helpers who are available.

title: Online Passport Renewal (OPR 3.0)
description: Focus led the engineering implementation of the newly launched online passport renewal system. We launched a mobile-first, accessible, USWDS-based application built and launched within 12 months. As part of this initiative, we also deployed the first cloud systems for the  consular division before transitioning it to a new division wide platform system. We continue to support this program through enhancements and expansion of supported use cases.

impact.headline: 2 million renewals in 5 months
impact.description: Replaced a paper-based, weeks-long process with a mobile-first, digital experience that meets citizens where they are.

impact.headline: 6 minutes to complete
impact.description: Average time for a citizen to renew their passport online, down from weeks by mail.

clientQuote.quote: The once-tedious all-day affair has never been faster or easier
clientQuote.name: Washington Post

title: New Jersey Unemployment Insurance
description: Supporting the large-scale modernization of New Jersey's unemployment insurance program, which was strained by pandemic-era claims and increased fraud. Collaborating with multiple vendors and agency stakeholders to improve the applicant experience and update the legacy mainframe to use modern APIs.

title: CDC ReportStream
client: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
description: Built as part of the COVID-19 pandemic response, this tool receives, routes, and delivers Covid test results to public health agencies. It was since expanded to support any kind of test results or orders across health data specifications including HL7 and FHIR. Focus supported this initiative acorss multiple teams including platform, portal, and integration onboarding.

title: Preventive Medicaid
description: Cityblock partners with states and their designated medicaid health insurer to provide preventive care for Medicaid patients. Focus delivered agile software development across engineering and cloud infrastructure teams, including the next iteration of their data pipeline platform and a client application for care providers to deliver community preventive care.

title: CMS Infrastructure as a Service
client: Center for Medicare and Medicaid Services
description: Supporting CMS's multi-year effort to modernize Medicare Fee for Service shared systems. Focus provides Infrastructure Engineering and Security Documentation services, applying common DevSecOps and site reliability practices across multiple development teams.

title: Skills-Based Talent Platform
description: Focus implemented a next-generation talent marketplace that uses well-defined, industry-aligned job competency maps to more accurately match job seekers with employers. The markplace was deployed at the Navajo nation to support workforce development initiatives aimed at brining businesses into the tribal reservation while encouraging residents to find well paying jobs locally.

title: Massachusetts Paid Family and Medical Leave
description: Focus was part of the initial core team for this legislatively mandated state program launched in 2020. Led delivery of critical milestones including the payments workflow, revenue data integration, approval workflow integration, and core API and cloud architecture. The program has paid over $4.5 billion in benefits since its launch in 2021.

impact.headline: $4.5 billion
impact.description: Total benefits distributed to Massachusetts workers since the program launched in 2021.

impact.headline: 350,000 employees
impact.description: Total number of Massachusets resident workers who applied for a paid leave benefit.

clientQuote.quote: Just submitted part 1 of my Mass PFML application for #FamilyLeave (easy+fast)! Can't wait to finally afford to take 12 weeks just being a dad, because on 1/1/21, new parents in Massachusets can get paid leave to bond with our kid!
clientQuote.name: Early user of MA Paid Family Leave (twitter/x)

title: Marriott Learning Platform
description: We built regional and organization-wide internal learning and development platforms emphasizing up-skilling and competency-based career growth for hotel staff.
